hero.headline: DPU-COL Online MBA in International Business Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #41 University 2025 | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹1,89,400

sections.tldr: DPU-COL Online MBA in International Business Management covers international marketing, international finance, global logistics and SCM, export-import procedures, international business law, and global strategic management. NAAC A++ NIRF #41. Total fee ₹1,89,400 with ₹10,000 discount. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. edX certifications bundled. UGC-DEB approved.

about.body:
DPU-COL's MBA in International Business Management covers the full cross-border business management stack: international marketing (market entry, adaptation, global branding), international finance (forex, international capital budgeting, trade finance), global logistics and supply chain management (international freight, customs, Incoterms), export-import procedures (DGFT, customs documentation, FEMA compliance), international business law (WTO dispute resolution, bilateral trade agreements), and global strategic management (MNC strategy, joint ventures, cross-cultural management).

The export-import procedures subject is particularly relevant for India's growing export ecosystem, covering DGFT licensing, customs documentation, Free Trade Agreements utilisation, and FEMA compliance for international transactions. This operational depth makes the specialisation directly applicable for professionals in trading companies, export houses, logistics firms, and MNC procurement teams.

Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune holds NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 (University 2025). edX certifications are bundled.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the International Business Management specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: International Marketing
subjects.desc: International market research, market entry strategies (exporting, licensing, JV, FDI), global branding, cultural adaptation in marketing, and international pricing strategies.

subjects.name: International Finance
subjects.desc: Foreign exchange markets, exchange rate determination and forecasting, international capital budgeting, trade finance instruments (LC, DP, DA), and forex hedging (forwards, options).

subjects.name: Global Logistics and Supply Chain Management
subjects.desc: International freight forwarding, Incoterms 2020 application, customs clearance process, containerisation and shipping logistics, and free trade zone operations.

subjects.name: Export-Import Procedures
subjects.desc: DGFT licensing and IEC code, customs documentation (shipping bill, bill of entry), Free Trade Agreement utilisation, FEMA compliance for international payments, and export promotion schemes.

subjects.name: International Business Law
subjects.desc: WTO dispute resolution, bilateral and multilateral trade agreements, CISG (UN Convention on Contracts), international intellectual property protection, and cross-border contract enforcement.

subjects.name: Global Strategic Management
subjects.desc: MNC organisational structures, global vs multi-domestic strategy, cross-cultural management frameworks, international JV and alliance management, and global competitive strategy.

subjects.name: Project Work
subjects.desc: Industry-linked international business project. Typically an export market feasibility study, international supply chain analysis, or FTA utilisation assessment.

whoHires.body:
DPU-COL MBA International Business Management graduates target roles in cross-border trade, MNC management, and global supply chains:

**Industries:** Export houses, trading companies, MNC India operations, international logistics and freight, DGFT and customs consultancy, international banking (trade finance), and government export promotion agencies

**Entry-level roles:** Export Executive, International Marketing Executive, Trade Finance Analyst, Customs Documentation Executive, Supply Chain Coordinator (international), Foreign Trade Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Export Manager, International Business Manager, Trade Finance Manager, Global Supply Chain Manager, Country Manager (MNC), EXIM Compliance Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP International Business, Head of Exports, Director of Global Supply Chain, Chief Commercial Officer (trading firms), Country Director (MNC)

**Employers:** Export houses and trading firms, IT services (international delivery management), pharma and chemical exporters, auto component exporters, logistics firms (DHL, Kuehne+Nagel, Maersk), and MNC India offices (P&G, Unilever, Siemens, Bosch).

**Note:** DPU-COL provides placement assistance for all MBA programmes.

skills.body:
The International Business Management specialisation builds skills across trade operations, global strategy, and cross-border compliance:

**Technical skills:**
- Export-Import Operations: IEC code application, shipping bill preparation, bill of entry processing, DGFT scheme utilisation (MEIS/RoDTEP), and customs duty calculation
- Trade Finance: letter of credit documentation, DP/DA collection process, export credit insurance, ECGC cover application, and FEMA compliance for outward/inward remittances
- International Logistics: Incoterms 2020 selection for different trade scenarios, container booking process, freight cost comparison (FCL vs LCL), and customs broker coordination
- Forex Management: forward contract booking, option premium evaluation, natural hedging strategies, and PCFC (Pre-Shipment Credit in Foreign Currency) utilisation

**Strategic skills:**
- International Market Entry: market screening methodology, entry mode selection framework, partner due diligence, and JV agreement negotiation
- Global Strategy: global integration vs local responsiveness trade-offs, international organisation design, and cross-cultural team management

**Soft skills:** Negotiating with international suppliers and buyers across cultural contexts, presenting export strategy to boards, managing multi-country logistics teams, and coordinating with customs authorities and DGFT officials.

comparisons.body:
Comparing DPU-COL Online MBA International Business Management against peer programmes:

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA International Business:** CU at ₹1,65,000 with NIRF #19. CU wins on NIRF rank and fee. DPU at ₹1,89,400 holds NAAC A++ (vs CU A+) and includes export-import procedures and international business law as standalone subjects.

**vs LPU Online MBA International Business:** LPU at approximately ₹2,00,000 with Coursera Plus. DPU saves ₹10,600 with stronger NAAC (A++ vs A+) and NIRF ranking.

**vs Symbiosis Online MBA International Business:** Symbiosis at approximately ₹1,70,000 with NIRF #20. Symbiosis wins on NIRF rank. DPU wins on NAAC grade (A++ vs A). Both are competitive for international business management roles.

**vs Amrita Online MBA (no IB spec):** Amrita at NIRF #8 does not list a dedicated International Business Management specialisation. For candidates targeting international trade and export management roles, DPU's dedicated spec is more directly relevant.

fees.body:
The DPU-COL Online MBA in International Business Management has a total programme fee of ₹1,89,400 for Indian students (USD 3,600 for international students).

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹50,000
- Semester 2: ₹50,000
- Semester 3: ₹45,000
- Semester 4: ₹44,400
- **Total: ₹1,89,400**

**Scholarship:** ₹10,000 off for full payment in one or two transactions within 15 days (effective fee ₹1,79,400).

**Payment options:**
- Zero-cost EMI: from ₹2,500/month directly through DPU-COL (no external NBFC, no interest)
- Semester-wise payments as above
- No application fee

**Bundled:** edX certifications included at no extra cost.

Fees are indicative. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com before payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with International Business Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune (DPU-COL) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #41 University rank. It is valid for government trade officer recruitment, PSU international operations roles, export promotion council positions, and MNC management recruitment. edX certifications supplement the credential. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from DPU-COL Online MBA International Business Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Sanjay T.
items.liked: Export-Import Procedures module was the most practically useful — DGFT licensing, customs documentation, and FEMA compliance are daily requirements in my trading firm
items.disliked: More coverage of digital trade platforms (ICEGATE, SWIFT GPI, eWay bill for exports) would modernise the operational content
items.body: I manage export operations at a chemical trading company. The EXIM procedures, trade finance, and international logistics subjects were directly applicable to my daily work. NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 satisfied the credential requirements for a senior export manager role I was targeting. The ₹10,000 lump-sum discount helped.

items.name: Priya V.
items.liked: International Finance covered forex hedging instruments at a practical level — forward contracts, options, and PCFC utilisation are directly applicable to my treasury role
items.disliked: International business law content leaned academic — more practical contract drafting and dispute resolution case studies would strengthen this module
items.body: I work in treasury at an auto component exporter. The international finance, EXIM procedures, and global logistics subjects gave me a holistic view of cross-border trade that my pure finance background lacked. DPU being in Pune with its strong export and manufacturing ecosystem made the programme contextually relevant.

items.name: Rakesh M.
items.liked: Global Strategic Management module helped me structure my company's market entry strategy for Southeast Asian markets
items.disliked: More India-specific FTA analysis (India-UAE CEPA, India-EFTA TEPA) would make the programme more directly actionable for Indian exporters
items.body: I run a small export house and needed the MBA for DGFT meetings and bank credit applications where management qualifications are assessed. The EXIM procedures, trade finance, and international marketing subjects directly improved my business operations. NAAC A++ from NIRF #41 satisfied every documentation requirement.

faqs.question: Does this MBA cover practical export-import procedures?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Export-Import Procedures subject covers IEC code application, shipping bill preparation, bill of entry processing, DGFT licensing and schemes, customs documentation, FTA utilisation, and FEMA compliance. This is operational-level content directly applicable for professionals in trading companies, export houses, and MNC procurement teams.

faqs.question: Is DPU International Business Management valid for trade officer and DGFT roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. DPU-COL's MBA is UGC-DEB approved from a NAAC A++ NIRF #41 university. It satisfies the MBA requirement for government trade officer recruitment, DGFT positions, export promotion council roles, and PSU international operations posts.

faqs.question: How does this differ from DPU's Finance MBA for export professionals?
faqs.answer: DPU's Finance MBA covers capital markets, corporate finance, and financial institutions broadly. International Business Management covers trade finance (LC, ECGC), forex management, EXIM procedures, and international logistics specifically. For professionals in export-import operations, international logistics, or MNC strategy, the International Business Management spec is more directly relevant.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹1,89,400 total (Sem 1: ₹50,000, Sem 2: ₹50,000, Sem 3: ₹45,000, Sem 4: ₹44,400). ₹10,000 scholarship for upfront payment within 15 days. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. No application fee. edX bundled. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover FTAs and trade agreements?
faqs.answer: Yes. International Business Law covers WTO dispute resolution, bilateral and multilateral trade agreements, and CISG. Export-Import Procedures covers FTA utilisation for customs duty reduction. The coverage is at management understanding level, sufficient for export managers and trade professionals to identify FTA opportunities and coordinate with customs brokers.

chooseIf: You work in export-import, international logistics, or MNC operations and need a specialised MBA.
DGFT meetings, bank credit applications, or trade promotion engagements require an MBA credential with NAAC A++ backing.
You need practical EXIM procedures, trade finance, and international logistics content in one MBA programme.
₹1,89,400 with zero-cost EMI and edX certifications fits your budget.
consider: NIRF top 20 matters — CU (NIRF #19) at ₹1,65,000 or Symbiosis (NIRF #20) at ₹1,70,000 are alternatives.
Your career target is purely domestic market management — a Marketing or Finance MBA would be more relevant.
You need deep country-specific market research skills — a dedicated international trade certification may supplement this MBA.
